claim: GitHub (or similar platforms like GitLab) is the future of AI coding orchestration—it provides issue specs, pull request validation, version control, and task assignment for multiple AI agents
actionable: Structure your AI coding workflow around GitHub: create issues as specs, assign to agents via comments, validate via PRs, and use GitHub Actions to orchestrate multiple coding agents in parallel
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)

claim: You can invoke multiple AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) on the same issue simultaneously via GitHub Actions by mentioning them with specific trigger keywords (e.g., @claude-fix, @cursor-fix)
actionable: Create GitHub Actions workflows for different coding agents; trigger them via issue/PR comments with keywords to let multiple agents work on the same task in parallel with isolated branches
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)

claim: Three architectural approaches exist for GitHub Actions AI coding: hybrid (agent creates branch/PR but you approve), deterministic (workflow handles branch/PR creation, agent only codes), and autonomous (agent handles everything)
actionable: Choose hybrid approach for validation/oversight, deterministic for control+speed, or autonomous for maximum efficiency; adjust based on your risk tolerance and codebase complexity
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)

claim: You can use Claude Code subscription tokens (via 'claude setup-token') instead of API keys in GitHub Actions, which is more cost-effective than per-API-call billing
actionable: Run 'claude setup-token' locally to get a subscription token that lasts 1 year; store it in GitHub secrets for use in Actions instead of paying per-API-call rates
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)

claim: GitHub Actions security requires restricting who can invoke AI coding assistants via role-based permission checks (only maintainers can trigger agents) to prevent unauthorized API usage
actionable: Add permission checks in your GitHub Actions workflows to only allow maintainers or specific users to invoke coding agents, preventing public repo abuse of your API quota
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)

claim: Branch naming conventions that include the coding agent's name and issue number (e.g., 'claude-issue-10') help organize and distinguish parallel agent work across the codebase
actionable: Define branch naming patterns in your workflow YAML that include both the agent name and issue number; this keeps parallel work organized and trackable
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)

claim: You can have multiple AI coding assistants review each other's pull requests via GitHub Actions (e.g., Claude reviews Cursor's PR, Cursor reviews Codex's PR) to improve validation coverage
actionable: Set up separate GitHub Actions workflows for each agent's review process; mention agents in PR comments (e.g., @claude-review) to have them review work from other agents
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)

claim: Integrating security scanning (e.g., Sonar MCP) into AI coding workflows before PR creation catches security issues early, reducing the need for human review
actionable: Add Sonar MCP (or similar security tools) to your GitHub Actions workflows to scan AI-generated code for security vulnerabilities before creating pull requests
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)

claim: The Cursor CLI supports headless mode with the '-p' flag for single-request execution; all major AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) have official GitHub Action integrations or CLI support
actionable: Use CLI tools like 'cursor -p <prompt>' for headless requests in GitHub Actions; leverage official GitHub Actions for each coding assistant for streamlined integration
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)

claim: Keeping global rules and agent instructions in a shared markdown file (e.g., agents.md) that's loaded by all workflows ensures consistent coding standards across all agents
actionable: Create an agents.md file with global coding standards, patterns, and instructions; reference it in all GitHub Actions workflows so every agent follows the same rules
source_title: GitHub is the Future of AI Coding (Here's Why)
